content: want to build a bin file to raspberrypi4b or rk chip, and develop some driver. In rust-dev branch, I saw that the arch-support.rst document says that aarch64 is supported, and how can I compile a Linux kernel for the aarch64 platform.

You can look at the Quick start: https://docs.kernel.org/rust/quick-start.html

The only difference with what's on the "Quick Start" is that you need to change the ARCH since it defaults to x86.

You can use these commands:

$ make LLVM=1 ARCH=arm64 defconfig rust.config
$ make LLVM=1 ARCH=arm64 rustavailable
Rust is available!
$ make LLVM=1 ARCH=arm64
Just change ARCH，and no need update change rust and c cross compile toolchain ？
About the C and Rust compilers, everything is explained in the Quick Start, there is nothing special to do for building the arm64 kernel besides the ARCH variable.
Thanks~:saluting_face:I'll have a try tonight
Thanks~:saluting_face:I'll have a try tonight
You may want to use one of the branch from the Rust-for-Linux repo. You can check which branch are available over there: https://rust-for-linux.com/branches.

is u-boot able to load this kernel ,
here is config.txt
kernel=Image

but the operating system is booting and not able to load the kernel

@Mahdi Guesmi: I don't see any reason why u-boot would not be able to load a kernel that has Rust enabled. The booting configuration is very dependent on which SoC you are using, if you have issues booting your own-built kernels on your boards, you should maybe contact the communities around your particular board and/or SoC.

Fabien Parent said:

Mahdi Guesmi: I don't see any reason why u-boot would not be able to load a kernel that has Rust enabled. The booting configuration is very dependent on which SoC you are using, if you have issues booting your own-built kernels on your boards, you should maybe contact the communities around your particular board and/or SoC.
``
well i have just setted the target in rustup for aarch64 and
then i did the rest of the steps to build a kernel using LLVM=1 and ARCH=arm64 without using Cross-compilation following the answer of someone in this comunity
after that i just built a normal image with u-boot kernel and rootfs for raspeberry linux and then i just changed the kernel to the kernel i have created that support rust , when the u-boot runs it prints starting to load kernel and then gets stuck there.
